tempssm 0.2.5
  • Reference
    • tempssm: State-space modeling for temperature time series in R
  • Changelog

Read and convert a monthly temperature CSV file to a ts object

Source: R/time_series_utilities.R
read_monthly_temp_ts.Rd

Read and convert a monthly temperature CSV file to a ts object

Usage

read_monthly_temp_ts(csv)

Arguments

csv

A length-one character string giving the path to a UTF-8 CSV file with columns Year, Month, and Temp.

Value

A univariate monthly ts object representing the temperature time series, with frequency = 12.

Details

The input CSV file must contain monthly temperature data with three columns: Year, Month, and Temp. The expected format is:


Year,Month,Temp
2001,1,10.4
2001,2,8.2
2001,3,NA
2001,4,13.6
...

The output is a regular monthly ts object. The Year and Month columns define calendar months, which are represented as equally spaced observations with frequency = 12. Month lengths are not converted to a fixed number of days.

Examples

## Create a temporary CSV file with monthly temperature data
tmp_csv <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")

writeLines(
  c(
    "Year,Month,Temp",
    "2001,1,10.4",
    "2001,2,8.2",
    "2001,3,NA",
    "2001,4,13.6",
    "2001,5,16.1"
  ),
  tmp_csv
)

## Read the CSV file and convert to a monthly ts object
temp_ts <- read_monthly_temp_ts(tmp_csv)
#> Loaded monthly temperature series with 5 observations

## Inspect the result
temp_ts
#>       Jan  Feb  Mar  Apr  May
#> 2001 10.4  8.2   NA 13.6 16.1
